annotations.label: Ring

anchor.snippet: equipped with two binary operations

annotations.note: `Ring` is defined as a `Semiring` with negation making it an additive group.

annotations.label: Commutative ring

anchor.snippet: is called commutative

annotations.note: `CommRing` extends `Ring` with commutative multiplication.

anchors.snippet: is called commutative

annotations.label: Ring of integers

anchor.snippet: is the ring of integers

annotations.note: `Int.instCommRing : CommRing ℤ` provides the commutative-ring structure on the integers.

annotations.label: Field

anchor.snippet: A field is a commutative ring where

annotations.note: `Field` is a `CommRing` that is also a `DivisionRing` (inverses for nonzero elements).

annotations.label: Polynomial ring

anchor.snippet: the set of all polynomials in the variable

annotations.note: `Polynomial R` is the univariate polynomial ring over `R` (multivariate is `MvPolynomial`).

annotations.label: Ring of continuous functions

anchor.snippet: complex-valued continuous functions on

annotations.note: `CommRing C(α, β)` gives the ring of continuous maps; taking `β = ℂ` yields complex-valued continuous functions.

annotations.label: Unit

anchor.snippet: is called a unit if it possesses a multiplicative inverse

annotations.note: `IsUnit a` states `a` equals some bundled unit `u : Mˣ`; `Units` (`Mˣ`) is the data version.

annotations.label: Zero divisor

anchor.snippet: Another particular type of element is the zero divisors

annotations.note: There is no standalone `IsZeroDivisor` predicate; Mathlib has the class `NoZeroDivisors` and the `nonZeroDivisors` submonoid capturing absence/membership of zero divisors.

annotations.label: Integral domain

anchor.snippet: it is called an integral domain (or domain)

annotations.note: `IsDomain` is a mixin extending `IsCancelMulZero` and `Nontrivial`; an integral domain is `[CommRing α] [IsDomain α]`.

annotations.label: Nilpotent element

anchor.snippet: is called nilpotent

annotations.note: `IsNilpotent x` is defined as `∃ n : ℕ, x ^ n = 0`.

annotations.label: Localization

anchor.snippet: The localization of a ring is a process in which some elements are rendered invertible

annotations.note: `IsLocalization M S` says `S` is the localization of `R` at submonoid `M`; the concrete construction is `Localization M`.

annotations.label: Quotient field

anchor.snippet: is a field, called the quotient field of

annotations.note: `IsFractionRing R K` abbreviates `IsLocalization (nonZeroDivisors R) K`; the concrete field is `FractionRing R`.

annotations.label: Ideals are two-sided

anchor.snippet: all ideals in a commutative ring are automatically two-sided

annotations.note: An instance gives every `Ideal α` over a CommSemiring/CommRing the `IsTwoSided` property, since `Ideal R := Submodule R R`.

annotations.label: Module

anchor.snippet: is like what a vector space is to a field

annotations.note: `Module R M` extends `DistribMulAction R M` with `add_smul` and `zero_smul`.

annotations.label: Free module

anchor.snippet: is called a free module

annotations.note: `Module.Free R M` is the class stating the `R`-module `M` admits a basis.

annotations.label: Submodule of free module need not be free

anchor.snippet: a submodule of a free module needs not to be free

annotations.note: No declaration stating this counterexample (a non-free submodule of a free module) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Module of finite type

anchor.snippet: A module of finite type is a module that has a finite spanning set

annotations.note: `Module.Finite R M` requires `(⊤ : Submodule R M).FG`, i.e. a finite spanning set.

annotations.label: Noetherian characterization via finite type

anchor.snippet: every submodule of a module of finite type is also of finite type

annotations.note: Both directions exist (`isNoetherian_of_isNoetherianRing_of_finite`, `IsNoetherian.finite`, `isNoetherian_def`), but the exact biconditional is not a single named theorem.

annotations.label: Ideal

anchor.snippet: are the submodules of

annotations.note: `Ideal R` is defined as the abbreviation `Submodule R R`.

annotations.label: Only ideals are zero and whole ring iff field

anchor.snippet: These two ideals are the only ones precisely if

annotations.note: `Ring.isField_iff_isSimpleOrder_ideal : IsField R ↔ IsSimpleOrder (Ideal R)` says `R` is a field iff `⊥` and `⊤` are the only ideals.

anchors.snippet: These two ideals are the only ones precisely if

annotations.label: Ideal generated by a set

anchor.snippet: is the smallest ideal that contains

annotations.note: `Ideal.span s := Submodule.span α s` is the smallest ideal containing the set `s`.

anchors.snippet: is the smallest ideal that contains

annotations.label: Principal ideal

anchor.snippet: Such an ideal is called a principal ideal

annotations.note: `Submodule.IsPrincipal S` is `∃ a, S = span R {a}`; applied to `Ideal R` it gives principal ideals.

annotations.label: Principal ideal ring

anchor.snippet: is called a principal ideal ring

annotations.note: `IsPrincipalIdealRing R` requires every ideal `S` to satisfy `S.IsPrincipal`.

annotations.label: Principal ideal domain

anchor.snippet: they are called principal ideal domains

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated PID class; a PID is expressed by combining `[IsDomain R]` and `[IsPrincipalIdealRing R]`.

annotations.label: PID is a UFD

anchor.snippet: is a unique factorization domain (UFD) which means that any element is a product of irreducible elements

annotations.note: Instance `PrincipalIdealRing.to_uniqueFactorizationMonoid` provides `UniqueFactorizationMonoid R` for a PID.

anchors.snippet: is a unique factorization domain (UFD) which means that any element is a product of irreducible elements

annotations.label: Irreducible element

anchor.snippet: in a domain is called irreducible if the only way of expressing it as a product

annotations.note: `Irreducible` is defined for elements of any monoid, generalizing the domain case.

anchors.snippet: in a domain is called irreducible if the only way of expressing it as a product

annotations.label: Irreducible polynomials

anchor.snippet: are irreducible polynomials

annotations.note: Irreducible polynomials are `Irreducible (p : Polynomial R)`, an invocation of the general predicate; there is no dedicated `Polynomial.Irreducible`.

anchors.snippet: are irreducible polynomials

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of arithmetic

anchor.snippet: It is also known as the fundamental theorem of arithmetic

annotations.note: `Nat.primeFactorsList_unique` proves uniqueness of prime factorization on ℕ; existence is `Nat.prod_primeFactorsList`.

anchors.snippet: It is also known as the fundamental theorem of arithmetic

annotations.label: Prime element

anchor.snippet: is a prime element if whenever

annotations.note: `Prime p` is nonzero, non-unit, and dividing a product implies dividing a factor.

annotations.label: Prime implies irreducible in a domain

anchor.snippet: being prime implies being irreducible

annotations.note: `Prime.irreducible` shows a prime element is irreducible over a `CommMonoidWithZero` with `IsCancelMulZero` (covering domains).

annotations.label: Factor ring

anchor.snippet: it is the set of cosets of

annotations.note: The quotient ring `R ⧸ I` carries a ring structure from `Ideal.Quotient.commRing`/`ring` instances.

anchors.snippet: it is the set of cosets of

annotations.label: Integers modulo n

anchor.snippet: is the ring of integers modulo

annotations.note: `ZMod n` is the integers modulo `n` (ℤ for 0, `Fin (n+1)` otherwise).

anchors.snippet: is the ring of integers modulo

annotations.label: Proper ideal

anchor.snippet: An ideal is proper if it is strictly smaller than the whole ring

annotations.note: There is no `Ideal.IsProper` definition; properness is expressed inline as `I ≠ ⊤`.

annotations.label: Maximal ideal

anchor.snippet: is not strictly contained in any proper ideal is called maximal

annotations.note: `Ideal.IsMaximal I` is defined as `IsCoatom I`.

annotations.label: Maximal ideal iff quotient is a field

anchor.snippet: is maximal if and only if

annotations.note: `Ideal.Quotient.maximal_ideal_iff_isField_quotient` states `I.IsMaximal ↔ IsField (R ⧸ I)`.

annotations.label: Existence of maximal ideals

anchor.snippet: any ring (with identity) possesses at least one maximal ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.exists_maximal` (Krull) gives `∃ M : Ideal α, M.IsMaximal` for any nontrivial (semi)ring.

annotations.label: Noetherian ring

anchor.snippet: A ring is called Noetherian

annotations.note: `IsNoetherianRing R := IsNoetherian R R`, i.e. all ideals are finitely generated.

anchors.snippet: A ring is called Noetherian

annotations.label: Hilbert basis theorem

anchor.snippet: then so is the polynomial ring

annotations.note: `Polynomial.isNoetherianRing` states `R[X]` is Noetherian when `R` is; `MvPolynomial.isNoetherianRing` covers finitely many variables.

annotations.label: Noetherian approximation

anchor.snippet: is the union of its Noetherian subrings

annotations.note: Only the building block (closure of a finite set is a Noetherian subring) exists; the union/colimit statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Artinian ring

anchor.snippet: A ring is called Artinian

annotations.note: `IsArtinianRing R := IsArtinian R R`, the descending chain condition on ideals.

anchors.snippet: A ring is called Artinian

annotations.label: Hopkins–Levitzki theorem

anchor.snippet: every Artinian ring is Noetherian

annotations.note: The general Hopkins–Levitzki theorem; the consequence 'Artinian ⇒ Noetherian' is an instance `[IsArtinianRing R] : IsNoetherianRing R` in the same file.

anchors.snippet: every Artinian ring is Noetherian

annotations.label: Artinian as zero-dimensional Noetherian

anchor.snippet: Artinian rings can be characterized as the Noetherian rings whose Krull dimension is zero

annotations.note: `IsArtinianRing R ↔ IsNoetherianRing R ∧ Ring.KrullDimLE 0 R` for a commutative ring.

anchors.snippet: Artinian rings can be characterized as the Noetherian rings whose Krull dimension is zero

annotations.label: Prime ideal

anchor.snippet: A prime ideal is a proper

annotations.note: `Ideal.IsPrime I` is a proper ideal with `ab ∈ I → a ∈ I ∨ b ∈ I`.

anchors.snippet: A prime ideal is a proper

annotations.label: Factorization into prime ideals in Dedekind rings

anchor.snippet: decomposes uniquely as a product of prime ideals

annotations.note: Instance `Ideal.uniqueFactorizationMonoid : UniqueFactorizationMonoid (Ideal A)` for a Dedekind domain gives unique factorization of ideals into primes.

anchors.snippet: decomposes uniquely as a product of prime ideals

annotations.label: Maximal ideals are prime

anchor.snippet: Any maximal ideal is a prime ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.IsMaximal.isPrime` turns a maximal ideal into a prime ideal.

annotations.label: Prime ideal iff quotient is a domain

anchor.snippet: is prime if and only if the factor ring

annotations.note: `isDomain_iff_prime : IsDomain (R ⧸ I) ↔ I.IsPrime`.

annotations.label: Local ring

anchor.snippet: Such rings are called local

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing` (nontrivial, `a` or `1-a` is a unit) is equivalent to having a unique maximal ideal.

annotations.label: Spectrum of a ring

anchor.snippet: is the set of all prime ideals of

annotations.note: `PrimeSpectrum` bundles an ideal together with a proof it is prime.

anchors.snippet: is the set of all prime ideals of

annotations.label: Maximal spectrum bijection (Nullstellensatz)

anchor.snippet: is in bijection with the set

annotations.note: Over an algebraically closed field with finitely many variables, maximal ideals correspond to singleton points, giving the bijection's content but no packaged `Equiv`.

annotations.label: Minimal primes and irreducible components

anchor.snippet: correspond to the irreducible components of

annotations.note: `minimalPrimes R ≃o (irreducibleComponents (PrimeSpectrum R))ᵒᵈ`, an order isomorphism.

annotations.label: Finitely many irreducible components for Noetherian Spec

anchor.snippet: has only finitely many irreducible components

annotations.note: A Noetherian topological space has finitely many irreducible components; `PrimeSpectrum` of a Noetherian ring is such a space.

annotations.label: Primary decomposition

anchor.snippet: any ideal can be decomposed as a product of finitely many primary ideals

annotations.note: Lasker–Noether: every submodule of a Noetherian module is a finite intersection of primary submodules; specializes to ideals (intersection, not product).

annotations.label: Affine scheme

anchor.snippet: is called an affine scheme

annotations.note: `AffineScheme` is the essential image of the `Spec` functor, with `IsAffine X` characterizing affine schemes.

annotations.label: Ring recovered as global sections

anchor.snippet: the underlying ring R can be recovered as the global sections

annotations.note: `ΓSpecIso` gives the isomorphism `Γ(Spec R, ⊤) ≅ R`, recovering `R` as global sections.

annotations.label: Krull dimension

anchor.snippet: The Krull dimension (or dimension)

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim R` is the Krull dimension of `PrimeSpectrum R`.

annotations.label: Dimension as supremum of prime chains

anchor.snippet: as the supremum of lengths n of chains of prime ideals

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim` is `krullDim (PrimeSpectrum R)`, where `Order.krullDim` is the supremum of lengths of chains of primes.

annotations.label: A field is zero-dimensional

anchor.snippet: a field is zero-dimensional

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field` states `ringKrullDim F = 0` for a field `F`.

annotations.label: Noetherian local rings have finite dimension

anchor.snippet: Noetherian local rings have finite dimension

annotations.note: Under `[CommRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsLocalRing R]`, an instance provides `FiniteRingKrullDim R`.

annotations.label: Ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: A ring homomorphism or, more colloquially, simply a map

annotations.note: `RingHom` (notation `→+*`) bundles `MonoidHom` and `AddMonoidHom` data of ring homomorphisms.

annotations.label: Kernel and image

anchor.snippet: The kernel and image of f are defined by

annotations.note: `RingHom.ker` is `Ideal.comap f ⊥`; `RingHom.range` is the image as a `Subring`.

annotations.label: Kernel is ideal, image is subring

anchor.snippet: The kernel is an ideal of R , and the image is a subring of S

annotations.note: `RingHom.ker` has type `Ideal R` and `RingHom.range` has type `Subring S` by definition, so this holds at the type level.

annotations.label: Ring isomorphism

anchor.snippet: is called an isomorphism if it is bijective

annotations.note: `RingEquiv` (notation `≃+*`) is the structure of bijective ring homomorphisms.

anchors.snippet: is called an isomorphism if it is bijective

annotations.label: Chinese remainder theorem

anchor.snippet: known as the Chinese remainder theorem

annotations.note: `quotientInfRingEquivPiQuotient` is the CRT ring isomorphism `(R ⧸ ⨅ i, f i) ≃+* ∀ i, R ⧸ f i` for pairwise coprime ideals.

anchors.snippet: known as the Chinese remainder theorem

annotations.label: Z is the initial commutative ring

anchor.snippet: The ring Z is the initial object in this category

annotations.note: `zIsInitial` proves `IsInitial (CommRingCat.of ℤ)` via the unique `Int.castRingHom`.

anchors.snippet: The ring Z is the initial object in this category

annotations.label: Binomial formula in commutative rings

anchor.snippet: which is valid for any two elements a and b in any commutative ring

annotations.note: `add_pow` is the binomial theorem over a `CommSemiring`; `Commute.add_pow` generalizes to commuting elements.

anchors.snippet: which is valid for any two elements a and b in any commutative ring

annotations.label: Tensor product of R-algebras

anchor.snippet: is again a commutative R -algebra

annotations.note: `instCommRing` gives `CommRing (A ⊗[R] B)` and `instAlgebra` the R-algebra structure, so the tensor product is a commutative R-algebra.

annotations.label: Finitely generated algebra

anchor.snippet: is called finitely generated (as an algebra) if

annotations.note: `Algebra.FiniteType` asserts `(⊤ : Subalgebra R A).FG`, i.e. `A` is finitely generated as an R-algebra.

annotations.label: Finitely generated module

anchor.snippet: S is finitely generated as an R -module

annotations.note: `Module.Finite` asserts `(⊤ : Submodule R M).FG`, i.e. the module is finitely generated.

annotations.label: Local ring

anchor.snippet: A ring is called local if it has only a single maximal ideal

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing` is defined via the unit condition; the unique-maximal-ideal characterization is `IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal`.

annotations.label: Localization at a prime is local

anchor.snippet: at a prime ideal p is local

annotations.note: `IsLocalization.AtPrime.isLocalRing` states a localization at a prime is a local ring.

annotations.label: Residue field

anchor.snippet: The residue field of R is defined as

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing.ResidueField` is `R ⧸ maximalIdeal R` with a `Field` instance.

annotations.label: Nakayama's lemma

anchor.snippet: Nakayama's lemma shows this passage is preserving important information

annotations.note: Nakayama's lemma appears in several forms, e.g. `Submodule.eq_smul_of_le_smul_of_le_jacobson` and `exists_sub_one_mem_and_smul_eq_zero_of_fg_of_le_smul`.

annotations.label: Cotangent space m/m^2

anchor.snippet: is an algebraic incarnation of the cotangent space

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` is `(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent`, i.e. m/m² as a residue-field vector space.

annotations.label: Dimension inequality for Noetherian local rings

anchor.snippet: For any Noetherian local ring R , the inequality

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal` gives dim R ≤ minimal generators of m, equal to finrank of the cotangent space.

annotations.label: Regular local ring

anchor.snippet: R is called a regular local ring

annotations.note: `IsRegularLocalRing` requires `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`, with an m/m² characterization.

anchors.snippet: R is called a regular local ring

annotations.label: Regularity iff associated graded is polynomial ring

anchor.snippet: A Noetherian local ring is regular if and only if the ring

annotations.note: No formalization of the associated graded ring gr_m(R) being a polynomial ring as a regularity criterion was found.

anchors.snippet: A Noetherian local ring is regular if and only if the ring

annotations.label: Regular local rings are UFDs

anchor.snippet: Regular local rings are UFD's

annotations.note: No Auslander–Buchsbaum-type theorem (`IsRegularLocalRing` implies `UniqueFactorizationMonoid`) was found in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Regular local rings are UFD's

annotations.label: Discrete valuation ring

anchor.snippet: Discrete valuation rings are equipped with a function

annotations.note: `IsDiscreteValuationRing` is a local PID domain that is not a field; the additive valuation `addVal R` is defined in the same file.

annotations.label: DVRs are one-dimensional regular local rings

anchor.snippet: Discrete valuation rings are precisely the one-dimensional regular local rings

annotations.note: `finrank_CotangentSpace_eq_one_iff` equates DVR with finrank(m/m²)=1 for a Noetherian local domain, but no lemma states DVR iff (regular ∧ dim 1) directly.

annotations.label: Germs of holomorphic functions on a Riemann surface

anchor.snippet: the ring of germs of holomorphic functions on a Riemann surface is a discrete valuation ring

annotations.note: No formalization of the ring of germs of holomorphic functions on a Riemann surface being a DVR was found.

annotations.label: Krull's principal ideal theorem dimension bound

anchor.snippet: is at least r − n

annotations.note: Krull's height theorem (`height ≤ n` for a prime minimal over n generators), with `height_le_one_of_isPrincipal...` as the Hauptidealsatz, is the ring-theoretic form of the codimension bound.

annotations.note: Grep for 'CompleteIntersection' returned no matches in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular local rings are complete intersections

anchor.snippet: Any regular local ring is a complete intersection ring, but not conversely

annotations.note: No complete-intersection notion exists in Mathlib, so this implication is not formalized.

anchor.snippet: A ring R is a set-theoretic complete intersection if

annotations.note: No notion of set-theoretic complete intersection was found in Mathlib.

annotations.note: `Mathlib/RingTheory/Regular/RegularSequence.lean` has regular sequences but lists depth as a TODO; no depth definition exists.

anchor.snippet: For any local Noetherian ring, the inequality

annotations.note: Depth is not defined in Mathlib, so the inequality depth ≤ dim cannot be stated.

annotations.label: Cohen–Macaulay ring

anchor.snippet: is called a Cohen–Macaulay ring

annotations.note: Grep for 'CohenMacaulay' across Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay

anchor.snippet: regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay, but not conversely

annotations.label: Normal ring

anchor.snippet: integrally closed in its field of fractions is called normal

annotations.note: `IsIntegrallyClosed R` is defined as `IsIntegrallyClosedIn R (FractionRing R)`, i.e. integrally closed in its fraction field.

annotations.label: Normal one-dimensional rings are regular

anchor.snippet: any normal one-dimensional ring is necessarily regular

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a Noetherian integrally closed domain of dim ≤ 1 is Dedekind with DVR localizations (and DVRs are regular), but no single 'normal dim-1 ⇒ regular' statement exists.

annotations.label: I-adic topology

anchor.snippet: This topology is called the I -adic topology

annotations.note: `Ideal.adicTopology` is the topology with powers of `I` as a basis of neighborhoods of zero.

annotations.label: I-adic completion

anchor.snippet: the I -adic completion is the inverse limit of the rings

annotations.note: `AdicCompletion I M` is the inverse limit of the quotients `M ⧸ (I^n • ⊤)`.

annotations.label: Complete ring

anchor.snippet: is isomorphic to its own completion, is called complete

annotations.note: `IsAdicComplete I M` asserts `M` is Hausdorff and precomplete with respect to `I`.

annotations.label: Hensel's lemma

anchor.snippet: Complete local rings satisfy Hensel's lemma

annotations.note: `IsAdicComplete.henselianRing` gives `HenselianRing R I` (Hensel's lemma) for I-adically complete rings.

annotations.label: Projective module

anchor.snippet: Projective modules can be defined to be the direct summands of free modules

annotations.note: `Module.Projective R P` is the standard projectivity predicate; equivalence with 'direct summand of free' is provided via `of_free` and `projective_def`.

annotations.label: Finitely generated projective over local ring is free

anchor.snippet: any finitely generated projective module is actually free

annotations.note: `free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing` proves a finite flat module over a local ring is free; finite projective modules are flat, generalizing the projective case.

annotations.label: Quillen–Suslin theorem

anchor.snippet: The Quillen–Suslin theorem asserts that any finitely generated projective module over

annotations.note: Grep for 'Quillen' only finds Quillen model-category material; no Quillen–Suslin theorem exists.

annotations.label: Regularity iff finite global dimension

anchor.snippet: A local Noetherian ring is regular if and only if its global dimension is finite

annotations.note: No global dimension definition exists in Mathlib, so Serre's characterization is absent.

annotations.label: Ext functor

anchor.snippet: This functor is the derived functor of the functor

annotations.note: `Ext R C n` is defined as the derived functor of the Hom bifunctor in an abelian category.

annotations.label: Regularity iff vanishing Ext

anchor.snippet: with residue field k is regular if and only if

annotations.note: No characterization of regularity via vanishing of Ext groups with the residue field is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Betti numbers and complete intersections

anchor.snippet: known as Betti numbers , grow polynomially in n if and only if

annotations.note: Grep for 'Betti' in this commutative-algebra sense finds nothing; no polynomial-growth/complete-intersection result exists.

annotations.label: Flat module

anchor.snippet: If it is exact, M is called flat

annotations.note: `Module.Flat` is the class of flat modules, defined via injectivity of `rTensor` on finitely generated submodules.

annotations.label: Finitely presented flat over local ring is free

anchor.snippet: any finitely presented flat module is free of finite rank

annotations.note: `free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing` states a finite flat module over a local ring is free.

annotations.label: Fiber dimension of flat algebras

anchor.snippet: if an R -algebra S is flat, the dimensions of the fibers

annotations.note: No dimension formula dim S = dim R + dim(fiber) for flat algebras was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wedderburn's little theorem

anchor.snippet: every finite division ring is commutative

annotations.note: `littleWedderburn` is an instance showing every finite `DivisionRing` is a `Field`, hence commutative.

annotations.label: Jacobson's commutativity condition

anchor.snippet: Another condition ensuring commutativity of a ring, due to Jacobson

annotations.note: No theorem deriving commutativity from `x^n(x) = x` (Jacobson's condition) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boolean ring

anchor.snippet: the ring is called Boolean ring

annotations.note: `BooleanRing` is the class of rings where every element is idempotent.

annotations.label: Graded-commutative ring

anchor.snippet: is called graded-commutative if, for all homogeneous elements

annotations.note: No general graded-commutative ring class with the sign rule was found; `GradedRing` exists but graded-commutativity appears only ad hoc.

annotations.label: Commutative differential graded algebra

anchor.snippet: R is called a commutative differential graded algebra (cdga)

annotations.note: No cdga or differential graded algebra definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential forms as a cdga

anchor.snippet: the complex of differential forms on a manifold

annotations.note: No de Rham complex of differential forms structured as a cdga was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cohomology of a cdga is graded-commutative

anchor.snippet: The cohomology of a cdga is a graded-commutative ring

annotations.note: Neither cdga nor graded-commutative ring is formalized, so this proposition is absent.

annotations.label: Superalgebra

anchor.snippet: is called a superalgebra

annotations.note: No `Superalgebra` definition exists; the term appears only as a comment in `CliffordAlgebra.Grading`.

annotations.label: Almost commutative ring

anchor.snippet: A related notion is an almost commutative ring

annotations.note: No almost commutative ring definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simplicial commutative ring

anchor.snippet: A simplicial commutative ring is a simplicial object in the category of commutative rings

annotations.note: `SimplicialObject C` is defined so `SimplicialObject CommRingCat` is expressible, but no dedicated 'simplicial commutative ring' definition exists.
